_patch_meta.motivation: Insert 12 Q-COV catalogue questions into wa_obs_question_catalogue per coverage-flags-redesign-v1-20260420.md §12.3. Questions surface into prose (Session A/B) when a VERSE_EVIDENCE_* flag is raised, driving AI-authored analytical answers that land as findings.

rows.question_text: Is this term's meaning covered by another term with similar meaning in the inner-being scope?
Is this term a modern concept / neologism with no substantive biblical antecedent?
Does the absence of biblical evidence itself have analytical significance for the inner-being picture?
Is this term simply not relevant to inner-being study? If so, should the term be deprecated from this registry?
Was STEP's verse capture exhausted for this term? Was any pagination or filter step incomplete?
What do the few verses that exist establish about the term's inner-being contribution?
Does the narrow verse set concentrate in a particular literary genre, historical period, or speaker?
VERSE_EVIDENCE_HIGH indicates that many verses in the Bible carry the same evidential value. Does the surrounding context of the verse provide any further differential value?
Does the high frequency repeat itself in both Old and New Testament? Is there any analytic value in this finding?
Does the high frequency correlate to the term's importance for understanding the inner being?
The term's usage in the verse has multiple analytic coverages - does the context around the verse impact the meaning so as to let us understand the different nuances better?
Has the breadth been captured in the inter-relationships and correlations with other terms and words?
